title: Immunofluorescence Microscopy Protocol with Methanol Fixed Cells(about S100A6 in SW480 Cells)
steps: The cells were grown on coverslips for 2 days in DMEM at 37°C and treated with corresponding pretreatment. At the time of fixation, cells should be ~50% confluent. Rinse cells briefly in 1X PBS. Fix cells with 4% cold paraformaldehyde for 20 min at room temperature. permeabilized for 10 min with 0.3% Triton X-100 at 37 °C. Rinse three times in 1X PBS, 5 minutes each. Block samples in 5% normal goat serum for 30 min at room temperature.
15.Chamber slides and coverslips. 16.Antibody dilution solution: 5% FBS in 1X PBS

7.Dilute the primary antibody to the recommended concentration/dilution in 5% FBS/PBS overnight at 4 ℃. 8.Rinse three times in 1X PBS, 5 minutes each. 9.Prepare FITC-conjugated secondary antibody in 5% FBS/PBS in the dark for 1 hour at room temperature according to the recommended manufacturer specification data sheet. 10.Nuclei were stained with DAPI for 5 min. 11.Rinse three times in 1X PBS, 5 minutes each. 12.Coverslip with anti‐fade mounting medium. 13.Seal the edges of the coverslip to the slide to prevent movement of the coverslip while imaging. One sealant that is recommended is a 1:1:1 ratio mixture of vasoline, lanolin and paraffin. 14.Immunofluorescence was analyzed with a META-510 Laser Scanning Confocal Imaging System.
creator: Shanshan Feng
